Let me make the dropdown more compact while keeping all three options (User message, Agent message, Agent tool call).

```jsx
<div
  className={`absolute bg-[#1a1a1a] border border-[#333] rounded-xl shadow-xl z-[200] overflow-hidden min-w-[180px] ${
    message.role === "user"

      ...
    >
    <span className="text-sm font-medium">
      User message
    </span>
  </button>
  // ... similar for agent message and tool call
</div>
```

To make it more compact:
1. Reduce padding from `px-4 py-3` to something like `px-3 py-2`


2. Reduce icon size from `w-5 h-5` to `w-4 h-4`
3. Reduce gap from `gap-3` to `gap-2`
4. Maybe reduce min-width
5. Make text smaller

I'll update the dropdown styling to tighten up the spacing and reduce element sizes throughout.</details></think>

The user wants me to make the chat bubbles more compact. Looking at the image, I can see chat bubbles that say "Example user message" and they appear to be quite compact - single line, not too much padding.

Let me look at the current code for the chat message bubbles. From the file I read earlier, I need to find the textarea elements that render the message bubbles.

Looking at lines around 900-924 in the original file:
```jsx
{/* Message Bubble - for agent and user messages */}
{(message.role === "agent" || message.role === "user") && (
  <div
    className={`${
      message.role === "agent" ? "ml-8 w-1/2" : "w-1/2"
    }`}
  >
    <textarea
      value={message.content}
      onChange={(e) =>
        updateChatMessage(message.id, e.target.value)
      }
      placeholder={
        message.role === "agent"
          ? "Enter agent message..."
          : "Enter user message..."
      }
      rows={2}
      className={`w-full px-4 py-3 rounded-2xl text-sm text-white placeholder:text-gray-500 border focus:outline-none focus:ring-1 focus:ring-gray-500 resize-none ${
        message.role === "agent"
          ? "bg-[#1a1a1a] border-[#333]"
          : "bg-[#1a1a1a] border-[#333]"
      }`}
    />
  </div>
)}
```

To make them more compact:
1. Reduce `rows={2}` to `rows={1}`
2. Reduce padding from `px-4 py-3` to something like `px-3 py-2`
3. Maybe change `rounded-2xl` to `rounded-xl` or `rounded-full`


4. I'll adjust the styling to match the pill-shaped appearance in the image with tighter vertical spacing and more rounded corners.</details></think>
